steps.title: Compare each month's housing starts from the table
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'April.': April had 1,803 thousand starts — the highest value in the table. ✓
  • Choice B — 'March.': March had 1,713 thousand starts — lower than both April and February. ✗
  • Choice C — 'January.': January had 1,669 thousand starts — the lowest value in the table. ✗
  • Choice D — 'February.': February had 1,771 thousand starts — second highest, but still below April. ✗

steps.title: Identify the maximum value
steps.content: Reading across the table: January 1,669 → February 1,771 → March 1,713 → April 1,803. The peak is 1,803, which falls in April. That matches choice A — A is correct.
